overview.tagline: A Great Place to Live, Work, and Play
overview.description: Fair Lawn is a diverse, middle-class borough of over 33,000 residents in central Bergen County. Bordered by the Passaic River to the south and Saddle River to the west, Fair Lawn developed rapidly after World War II with the Radburn planned community, one of the first neighborhoods in America designed around the automobile. Today, Fair Lawn is known for its excellent schools, large Orthodox Jewish community, diverse dining scene, and convenient NJ Transit train service to Manhattan.
highlights: Home to the historic Radburn community — America's first planned 'Town for the Motor Age'
NJ Transit train station on the Bergen County Line with direct service to Hoboken
Large and active Orthodox Jewish community with numerous synagogues and kosher dining
Highly rated public school system with strong academics and athletics
Diverse population with excellent Korean, Middle Eastern, and kosher restaurants

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Fair Lawn features diverse housing stock ranging from pre-war homes in Radburn to post-war Cape Cods and colonials throughout the borough.
realEstate.recentTrends: Fair Lawn's market is competitive, driven by good schools, train access, and relative affordability compared to northern Bergen towns. Homes in the Radburn section and near the train station are especially sought after. Many older homes are being renovated or replaced with larger new construction.
neighborhoodNotes: Radburn is the most distinctive neighborhood — a planned community with interior parks, pedestrian underpasses, and a swim club
The Fair Lawn Avenue corridor is the main commercial district with shops and restaurants
The Saddle River area on the western border features larger lots and Saddle River County Park access
The area near Broadway is popular with the Orthodox Jewish community and features kosher restaurants and shops
The 'Warren Point' section near the train station commands premium prices for commuter convenience

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by the Borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by neighborhood — check the borough website or app
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use sealed bags or covered containers not exceeding 50 lbs
Bulk item pickup available by appointment with the DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream recycling is collected weekly through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables (plastic, glass, cans) collected in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected on a separate schedule
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-soiled items
Yard waste collected seasonally; check DPW schedule

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water (formerly United Water) and sewer service is managed through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills issued quarterly by Veolia
Sewer charges appear on property tax or utility bills
Water quality reports published annually
Report water emergencies to Veolia or DPW

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Fair Lawn Building Department handles construction, renovation, and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for structural, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC work
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board or Zoning Board of Adjustment
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and changes of use
Radburn section has additional architectural review requirements

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Fair Lawn manages municipal parking lots and street parking, including commuter parking near the train station.
details: Commuter parking available at the Fair Lawn train station with permit
Metered parking in the downtown commercial district
Residential permit parking in areas near the train station
Free parking in many neighborhood commercial areas

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Recreation Department offers extensive youth and adult programs throughout the year.
details: Youth sports leagues including baseball, soccer, basketball, lacrosse, and football
Summer recreation program and day camps
Adult fitness programs and sports leagues
Community Center and Memorial Pool facility

additionalServices.title: Senior Services
additionalServices.description: Fair Lawn Senior Center provides programs and services for residents aged 60+.
details: Located at the Fair Lawn Senior Center on Fair Lawn Avenue
Daily activities, exercise programs, and social events
Transportation to medical appointments and shopping
Hot meal program and special event trips

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Fair Lawn enforces noise restrictions across its residential and commercial areas.
keyRules: Construction work prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Power equipment (leaf blowers, mowers) restricted to 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM
Excessive noise from residential properties subject to fines

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in accordance with borough standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be kept trimmed — grass over 10 inches may result in a violation
Snow and ice must be cleared from sidewalks within 24 hours of storm ending
No accumulation of trash, debris, or abandoned vehicles on properties
Fences must comply with height and setback requirements
Radburn residents must also comply with Radburn Association rules

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed annually and controlled at all times within the borough.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed with the Borough Clerk by January 31 each year
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Owners must clean up after their pets on public and private property
Rabies vaccination required for all dogs and cats
Dog park available at the Fair Lawn Community Center

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Fair Lawn enforces parking regulations on residential and commercial streets.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets (2:00 AM to 6:00 AM) without a permit
Alternate-side parking enforced for street sweeping on designated streets
Commuter parking at the train station requires a borough permit
Fire hydrant clearance of 10 feet required at all times
Snow emergency parking bans enforced when declared

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Fair Lawn regulates rental properties to maintain housing standards.
keyRules: Landlords must register rental properties with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before tenant occupancy
Fire safety inspections required for multi-family buildings
Security deposit limited to 1.5 months' rent per NJ state law
Tenants protected under NJ Anti-Eviction Act
rentals.source: Fair Lawn Borough Code, NJ Landlord-Tenant Law

other.title: Eruv
other.summary: Fair Lawn has an established eruv (symbolic boundary) for the Orthodox Jewish community.
keyRules: The eruv is maintained by the local Orthodox community and is checked weekly
Residents should not interfere with eruv poles or wires
Borough cooperates with eruv maintenance on public utility poles
Status of the eruv announced weekly by local synagogues

publicSchools.name: Fair Lawn High School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive high school with strong AP program, award-winning performing arts, and competitive athletics (the Cutters)

publicSchools.name: Thomas Jefferson Middle School
publicSchools.notes: One of two middle schools; offers honors and enrichment programs

publicSchools.name: Memorial Middle School
publicSchools.notes: One of two middle schools serving Fair Lawn students

publicSchools.name: Radburn School
publicSchools.notes: Located in the historic Radburn neighborhood

privateSchools.name: St. Anne School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school serving Fair Lawn and surrounding communities

privateSchools.name: Yavneh Academy
privateSchools.notes: Modern Orthodox Jewish day school located in neighboring Paramus, serving many Fair Lawn families

whyMoveHere: NJ Transit train station providing direct service to Hoboken and connections to NYC
Highly rated public schools with strong academics and extracurriculars
Active and welcoming Orthodox Jewish community with synagogues, kosher dining, and eruv
Historic Radburn neighborhood — a landmark in American urban planning
Diverse, affordable community with excellent highway and transit access

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Train (Bergen County Line) / Bus
commuteToNYC.duration: 40-55 minutes by train to Hoboken + PATH; 35-55 minutes by bus
commuteToNYC.notes: Fair Lawn has two NJ Transit train stations (Broadway and Radburn) on the Bergen County Line. Trains run to Hoboken where riders transfer to the PATH for Manhattan. NJ Transit bus routes also serve the Port Authority Bus Terminal. Car commute via Routes 4 or 208 to the GWB.

costOfLiving.summary: Fair Lawn offers a moderate cost of living for Bergen County, with solid housing values and good schools justifying property tax levels.

pros: Two NJ Transit train stations for NYC commuters
Diverse community with rich cultural offerings
Radburn neighborhood is a unique, historic planned community
More affordable than many northern Bergen County towns
cons: Train commute to Manhattan requires a transfer at Hoboken
Route 4 and Broadway traffic can be heavy during rush hours
Some areas near the rivers have flood risk
Aging housing stock in many neighborhoods requires updates

restaurants.name: The River Palm Terrace
restaurants.description: Upscale steakhouse with a sophisticated atmosphere, USDA prime cuts, fresh seafood, and an award-winning wine list.

restaurants.name: Plaza Grill House
restaurants.description: Popular kosher steakhouse offering high-quality grilled meats, sides, and a welcoming atmosphere for the observant community.

restaurants.name: Koreana
restaurants.description: Authentic Korean restaurant serving barbecue, bibimbap, and traditional Korean dishes with a loyal following.

restaurants.name: Roma Pizza
restaurants.description: Beloved local pizzeria serving New Jersey-style pizza, pasta, and Italian subs for decades.
restaurants.address: Fair Lawn Avenue, Fair Lawn, NJ 07410

restaurants.name: Hummus Elite
restaurants.description: Kosher Mediterranean restaurant featuring fresh hummus, falafel, shawarma, and Israeli-inspired dishes.

parks.name: Radburn Association Parks
parks.description: The historic Radburn neighborhood features a network of interior parks, swimming pools, and walking paths designed as part of America's first planned community.

parks.name: Saddle River County Park — Fair Lawn Section
parks.description: Bergen County park along the Saddle River with paved walking and biking paths, sports fields, and picnic areas.
parks.address: Saddle River Road, Fair Lawn, NJ 07410

parks.name: Memorial Park
parks.description: Community park with ball fields, a playground, a pool, basketball courts, and a memorial honoring local veterans.
parks.address: Fair Lawn Avenue, Fair Lawn, NJ 07410

parks.name: Fair Lawn Athletic Field Complex
parks.description: Multi-field sports complex used for youth and adult leagues, community events, and school athletics.

events.name: Fair Lawn Street Fair
events.description: Massive annual street fair along Fair Lawn Avenue with hundreds of vendors, food trucks, live entertainment, and carnival rides.

events.name: Oldies Concert in the Park
events.description: Summer concert series in Memorial Park featuring classic rock and oldies bands with food and family activities.

events.name: Independence Day Fireworks
events.description: Annual July 4th fireworks display at Memorial Park with food vendors and pre-show entertainment.

events.name: Holiday Festival
events.description: Annual holiday celebration with tree lighting, menorah lighting, live entertainment, and visits from Santa.

attractions.name: Historic Radburn
attractions.description: America's first planned community, designed in 1929 with cul-de-sacs, interior parks, and pedestrian underpasses — a National Historic Landmark in urban planning.

attractions.name: Fair Lawn Public Library
attractions.description: Modern public library offering extensive programs, meeting spaces, children's activities, and digital resources.
attractions.address: 10-01 Fair Lawn Avenue, Fair Lawn, NJ 07410

attractions.name: Garretson Forge and Farm
attractions.description: Historic Dutch-Colonial homestead dating to the 18th century, preserved as a museum by the Fair Lawn Historical Society.

shoppingAreas: Fair Lawn Avenue — Main commercial corridor with shops, restaurants, and local businesses
Broadway — Diverse shopping strip with kosher restaurants, bakeries, and specialty shops
Route 4 — Major commercial corridor with retail plazas and chain restaurants
River Road — Additional commercial strip with restaurants and services

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Fair Lawn Borough Hall, 8-01 Fair Lawn Avenue, Fair Lawn, NJ 07410

police.address: 8-01 Fair Lawn Avenue, Fair Lawn, NJ 07410

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450
